summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #447 where IPv6 bind addresses caused unparseable SocketAddr strings, then iterate on PR review comments until merged.
summary.outcome: Fixed all four IPv6 bind address formatting bugs across gateway, tls, courier, and oauth crates using IpAddr::parse + SocketAddr::new; PR #481 created, all review conversations resolved, and a nightly rustfmt formatting issue fixed.

repo: The tls crate uses a custom Context trait via impl_context!() macro rather than anyhow::Context — it still provides .with_context() but the source is crates/tls/src/error.rs
The existing format_url_host helper in crates/tls/src/lib.rs:280 already handles IPv6 bracket-wrapping for display/URL purposes — distinct from bind address formatting
The oauth callback server (crates/oauth/src/callback_server.rs) is localhost-only for local OAuth redirects, so IPv6 is low-risk there but was fixed for consistency
The project uses nightly-2025-11-30 rustfmt with stricter line-length rules that differ from stable — a two-line let binding was required to be collapsed to one line
CI runs format-check via a Justfile recipe `format-check` on line 14

code.finding: Main gateway bind address construction — was format!("{bind}:{port}"), fixed to IpAddr::parse + SocketAddr::new with map_err for error context
HTTP redirect server bind — same IPv6 formatting bug, fixed with with_context to include bind value in error
Courier service bind — same IPv6 formatting bug, fixed with anyhow::Context (already imported)
OAuth callback server bind — same bug, uses custom Error::message(), error message includes bind_addr value; nightly rustfmt requires single-line TcpListener::bind call

Test ipv6_bind_addresses_parse_correctly added after display_host_uses_real_ip_for_unspecified_bind — covers ::, ::1, 0.0.0.0, 127.0.0.1, fe80::1, plus negative assertions that :::8080 and ::1:8080 fail to parse

workflow: Greptile bot posted review comments on the PR — all were substantive (error messages missing bind value, weak test assertions, missing clarifying comment)
GitHub GraphQL API needed for resolving review threads — gh CLI alone doesn't expose thread resolution; requires pullRequestReviewThread node IDs and resolveReviewThread mutation
Running cargo check on just the affected workspace members (--package flags) is faster than a full workspace build for verifying targeted fixes
Nightly rustfmt formatting differences from stable can cause CI failures even when code compiles and tests pass — always run fmt check before pushing

friction: Beads issue tracker DB was in a broken state on the worktree — skipped creating a local issue
anyhow::Context import situation was ambiguous in tls crate — required reading error.rs and tracing the impl_context! macro to confirm .with_context() was available
The nightly rustfmt collapse of a two-line let binding to one line was only caught by CI, not by a local fmt check before pushing — caused an extra commit/push cycle
Multiple rounds of greptile bot review comments required three separate fix-and-resolve cycles
open_items: fe80::1 (link-local IPv6) bind address requires a zone ID (e.g. fe80::1%eth0) to actually bind — the test covers parsing but a comment was added noting this limitation; no runtime validation was added
The OAuth callback server is localhost-only but now accepts any bind address string — no validation that it's actually a loopback address was added
